question: What is our total revenue by quarter for 2024?
expected_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('quarter', o.order_date) AS quarter, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.order_date >= DATE '2024-01-01' AND o.order_date < DATE '2025-01-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and len(results) <= 4 and all('revenue' in r for r in results)

question: Which product categories are growing fastest?
expected_sql: WITH yearly AS (SELECT p.category, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.order_date)::INT AS yr, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id WHERE o.order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '2 years' GROUP BY 1, 2) SELECT y24.category, ROUND(((y24.revenue - y23.revenue) / NULLIF(y23.revenue, 0)) * 100, 2) AS growth_pct FROM yearly y24 JOIN yearly y23 ON y24.category = y23.category AND y24.yr = y23.yr + 1 ORDER BY growth_pct DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('category' in r for r in results)

question: Show me our top 10 customers by lifetime value
expected_sql: SELECT c.id AS customer_id, c.name, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS lifetime_value FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY lifetime_value DESC LIMIT 10
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 10 and all('lifetime_value' in r for r in results)

question: What is our customer retention rate by cohort?
expected_sql: WITH first_order AS (SELECT o.customer_id, MIN(DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date)) AS cohort_month FROM orders o GROUP BY o.customer_id), cohort_orders AS (SELECT fo.cohort_month, DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) AS active_month, COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) AS active_customers FROM first_order fo JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = fo.customer_id GROUP BY 1, 2), cohort_size AS (SELECT cohort_month, COUNT(*) AS cohort_customers FROM first_order GROUP BY 1) SELECT co.cohort_month, co.active_month, ROUND((co.active_customers::NUMERIC / NULLIF(cs.cohort_customers, 0)) * 100, 2) AS retention_rate_pct FROM cohort_orders co JOIN cohort_size cs ON cs.cohort_month = co.cohort_month ORDER BY 1, 2
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('retention_rate_pct' in r for r in results)

question: Which cities generate the most revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT c.city, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 10 and all('city' in r and 'revenue' in r for r in results)

question: How does average order value differ by customer segment?
expected_sql: WITH order_totals AS (SELECT o.id AS order_id, c.segment, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)) AS order_value FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id, c.segment) SELECT segment, ROUND(AVG(order_value), 2) AS avg_order_value FROM order_totals GROUP BY segment ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('avg_order_value' in r for r in results)

question: What is our return rate by product category?
expected_sql: WITH sold AS (SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS sold_items FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.category), returned AS (SELECT p.category, COUNT(*) AS returned_items FROM returns r JOIN order_items oi ON oi.id = r.order_item_id JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.category) SELECT s.category, ROUND((COALESCE(r.returned_items, 0)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(s.sold_items, 0)) * 100, 2) AS return_rate_pct FROM sold s LEFT JOIN returned r ON r.category = s.category ORDER BY return_rate_pct DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('return_rate_pct' in r for r in results)

question: What is the average shipping time by ship mode?
expected_sql: SELECT o.ship_mode, ROUND(AVG((o.ship_date - o.order_date)), 2) AS avg_ship_days FROM orders o GROUP BY o.ship_mode ORDER BY avg_ship_days ASC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('avg_ship_days' in r for r in results)

question: Show orders with discounts over 20% and their profit margins
expected_sql: SELECT o.id AS order_id, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue, ROUND(SUM((oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0) - p.cost) * oi.quantity), 2) AS profit, ROUND((SUM((oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0) - p.cost) * oi.quantity) / NULLIF(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 0)) * 100, 2) AS profit_margin_pct FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id WHERE oi.discount > 20 GROUP BY o.id ORDER BY profit_margin_pct DESC LIMIT 100
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('profit_margin_pct' in r for r in results)

question: Show monthly revenue trend for the last 12 months
expected_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) AS month, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.order_date >= DATE_TRUNC('month', CURRENT_DATE) - INTERVAL '11 months' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and len(results) <= 12 and all('revenue' in r for r in results)

question: What are the top 10 products by revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT p.id AS product_id, p.name, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id GROUP BY p.id, p.name ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 10 and all('revenue' in r for r in results)

question: How many orders do we have by status?
expected_sql: SELECT o.status, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders o GROUP BY o.status ORDER BY order_count DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('order_count' in r for r in results)

question: How many customers are in each segment?
expected_sql: SELECT c.segment, COUNT(*) AS customer_count FROM customers c GROUP BY c.segment ORDER BY customer_count DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and sum(r['customer_count'] for r in results) > 0

question: How many new customers signed up each month in 2024?
expected_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', c.signup_date) AS month, COUNT(*) AS new_customers FROM customers c WHERE c.signup_date >= DATE '2024-01-01' AND c.signup_date < DATE '2025-01-01' GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and len(results) <= 12

question: What is the average discount by product category?
expected_sql: SELECT p.category, ROUND(AVG(oi.discount), 2) AS avg_discount_pct FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY p.category ORDER BY avg_discount_pct DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('avg_discount_pct' in r for r in results)

question: Which sub-categories generate the most profit?
expected_sql: SELECT p.sub_category, ROUND(SUM((oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0) - p.cost) * oi.quantity), 2) AS profit FROM order_items oi JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id GROUP BY p.sub_category ORDER BY profit DESC LIMIT 15
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('profit' in r for r in results)

question: What is revenue by state?
expected_sql: SELECT c.state, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY c.state ORDER BY revenue DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('state' in r and 'revenue' in r for r in results)

question: What percentage of customers are repeat buyers?
expected_sql: WITH order_counts AS (SELECT o.customer_id, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders o GROUP BY o.customer_id) SELECT ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN order_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) * 100, 2) AS repeat_customer_rate_pct FROM order_counts
expected_result_check: len(results) == 1 and 'repeat_customer_rate_pct' in results[0]

question: What is our cancellation rate by month?
expected_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) AS month, ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN o.status = 'Cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) * 100, 2) AS cancellation_rate_pct FROM orders o GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('cancellation_rate_pct' in r for r in results)

question: What share of orders use same-day shipping?
expected_sql: SELECT ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN o.ship_mode = 'Same Day' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) * 100, 2) AS same_day_share_pct FROM orders o
expected_result_check: len(results) == 1 and 'same_day_share_pct' in results[0]

question: Which brands generate the most revenue?
expected_sql: SELECT p.brand, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id GROUP BY p.brand ORDER BY revenue DESC LIMIT 10
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 10 and all('brand' in r for r in results)

question: What is the median order value?
expected_sql: WITH order_totals AS (SELECT o.id, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)) AS order_value FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id) SELECT ROUND(PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY order_value), 2) AS median_order_value FROM order_totals
expected_result_check: len(results) == 1 and 'median_order_value' in results[0]

question: How many items are in an average order?
expected_sql: WITH items_per_order AS (SELECT oi.order_id, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_items FROM order_items oi GROUP BY oi.order_id) SELECT ROUND(AVG(total_items), 2) AS avg_items_per_order FROM items_per_order
expected_result_check: len(results) == 1 and 'avg_items_per_order' in results[0]

question: What is the distribution of return reasons?
expected_sql: SELECT r.reason, COUNT(*) AS return_count FROM returns r GROUP BY r.reason ORDER BY return_count DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('reason' in r for r in results)

question: How many orders took more than 7 days to ship?
expected_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS delayed_orders FROM orders o WHERE (o.ship_date - o.order_date) > 7
expected_result_check: len(results) == 1 and 'delayed_orders' in results[0]

question: Show customer lifetime orders and revenue
expected_sql: SELECT c.id AS customer_id, c.name, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS total_orders, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS lifetime_revenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY lifetime_revenue DESC LIMIT 25
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('total_orders' in r and 'lifetime_revenue' in r for r in results)

question: Which cities have the highest order count?
expected_sql: SELECT c.city, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 10
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 10 and all('order_count' in r for r in results)

question: Compare weekend vs weekday revenue
expected_sql: SELECT CASE WHEN EXTRACT(ISODOW FROM o.order_date) IN (6, 7) THEN 'weekend' ELSE 'weekday' END AS day_type, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY revenue DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and len(results) <= 2 and all('revenue' in r for r in results)

question: What is quarter-over-quarter revenue growth?
expected_sql: WITH q AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('quarter', o.order_date) AS quarter, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY 1) SELECT quarter, ROUND(revenue, 2) AS revenue, ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY quarter)) / NULLIF(LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY quarter), 0)) * 100, 2) AS qoq_growth_pct FROM q ORDER BY quarter
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('qoq_growth_pct' in r for r in results)

question: Show the top 20 orders by discount amount
expected_sql: SELECT o.id AS order_id, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS discount_amount FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id ORDER BY discount_amount DESC LIMIT 20
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 20 and all('discount_amount' in r for r in results)

question: What is monthly revenue by customer segment?
expected_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) AS month, c.segment, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('segment' in r and 'revenue' in r for r in results)

question: Which products have never been sold?
expected_sql: SELECT p.id AS product_id, p.name FROM products p LEFT JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = p.id WHERE oi.id IS NULL ORDER BY p.id LIMIT 100
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 0

question: Which products have the lowest gross margin?
expected_sql: SELECT p.id AS product_id, p.name, ROUND(((p.price - p.cost) / NULLIF(p.price, 0)) * 100, 2) AS gross_margin_pct FROM products p ORDER BY gross_margin_pct ASC LIMIT 20
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 20 and all('gross_margin_pct' in r for r in results)

question: What is profit margin by brand?
expected_sql: SELECT p.brand, ROUND((SUM((oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0) - p.cost) * oi.quantity) / NULLIF(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 0)) * 100, 2) AS profit_margin_pct FROM products p JOIN order_items oi ON oi.product_id = p.id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id GROUP BY p.brand ORDER BY profit_margin_pct DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('profit_margin_pct' in r for r in results)

question: How much revenue comes from each ship mode?
expected_sql: SELECT o.ship_mode, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY o.ship_mode ORDER BY revenue DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('ship_mode' in r and 'revenue' in r for r in results)

question: Which customer segments have the highest return rates?
expected_sql: WITH sold AS (SELECT c.segment, COUNT(*) AS sold_items FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY c.segment), returned AS (SELECT c.segment, COUNT(*) AS returned_items FROM returns r JOIN order_items oi ON oi.id = r.order_item_id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id JOIN customers c ON c.id = o.customer_id GROUP BY c.segment) SELECT s.segment, ROUND((COALESCE(r.returned_items, 0)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(s.sold_items, 0)) * 100, 2) AS return_rate_pct FROM sold s LEFT JOIN returned r ON r.segment = s.segment ORDER BY return_rate_pct DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('return_rate_pct' in r for r in results)

question: How long does it take to return items by reason?
expected_sql: SELECT r.reason, ROUND(AVG(r.return_date - o.order_date), 2) AS avg_days_to_return FROM returns r JOIN order_items oi ON oi.id = r.order_item_id JOIN orders o ON o.id = oi.order_id GROUP BY r.reason ORDER BY avg_days_to_return DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('avg_days_to_return' in r for r in results)

question: How many unique customers order each month?
expected_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) AS month, COUNT(DISTINCT o.customer_id) AS active_customers FROM orders o GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('active_customers' in r for r in results)

question: What is repeat purchase rate by signup quarter?
expected_sql: WITH customer_orders AS (SELECT c.id AS customer_id, DATE_TRUNC('quarter', c.signup_date) AS signup_quarter, COUNT(o.id) AS orders_count FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, DATE_TRUNC('quarter', c.signup_date)) SELECT signup_quarter, ROUND((SUM(CASE WHEN orders_count > 1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) * 100, 2) AS repeat_purchase_rate_pct FROM customer_orders GROUP BY signup_quarter ORDER BY signup_quarter
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('repeat_purchase_rate_pct' in r for r in results)

question: What is average discount by city for the top 20 cities by order volume?
expected_sql: WITH top_cities AS (SELECT c.city, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS order_count FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.city ORDER BY order_count DESC LIMIT 20) SELECT tc.city, ROUND(AVG(oi.discount), 2) AS avg_discount_pct FROM top_cities tc JOIN customers c ON c.city = tc.city JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY tc.city ORDER BY avg_discount_pct DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 20 and all('avg_discount_pct' in r for r in results)

question: Show revenue by category and quarter
expected_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('quarter', o.order_date) AS quarter, p.category, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('category' in r and 'revenue' in r for r in results)

question: How many high-value orders over $2000 do we have?
expected_sql: WITH order_totals AS (SELECT o.id, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)) AS order_value FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id) SELECT COUNT(*) AS high_value_orders FROM order_totals WHERE order_value > 2000
expected_result_check: len(results) == 1 and 'high_value_orders' in results[0]

question: Who are our top customers in the last 90 days?
expected_sql: SELECT c.id AS customer_id, c.name, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue_90d FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id WHERE o.order_date >= CURRENT_DATE - INTERVAL '90 days' GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY revenue_90d DESC LIMIT 10
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 10 and all('revenue_90d' in r for r in results)

question: Which products have the highest return rates?
expected_sql: WITH sold AS (SELECT oi.product_id, COUNT(*) AS sold_items FROM order_items oi GROUP BY oi.product_id), returned AS (SELECT oi.product_id, COUNT(*) AS returned_items FROM returns r JOIN order_items oi ON oi.id = r.order_item_id GROUP BY oi.product_id) SELECT p.id AS product_id, p.name, ROUND((COALESCE(r.returned_items, 0)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(s.sold_items, 0)) * 100, 2) AS return_rate_pct FROM sold s JOIN products p ON p.id = s.product_id LEFT JOIN returned r ON r.product_id = s.product_id ORDER BY return_rate_pct DESC LIMIT 20
expected_result_check: len(results) <= 20 and all('return_rate_pct' in r for r in results)

question: What is average order value by state?
expected_sql: WITH order_totals AS (SELECT o.id AS order_id, c.state, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)) AS order_value FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY o.id, c.state) SELECT state, ROUND(AVG(order_value), 2) AS avg_order_value FROM order_totals GROUP BY state ORDER BY avg_order_value DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('avg_order_value' in r for r in results)

question: Show order volume by day of week
expected_sql: SELECT TO_CHAR(o.order_date, 'Dy') AS day_of_week, COUNT(*) AS order_count FROM orders o GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY order_count DESC
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and len(results) <= 7

question: Which customer has been inactive the longest?
expected_sql: SELECT c.id AS customer_id, c.name, MAX(o.order_date) AS last_order_date FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON o.customer_id = c.id GROUP BY c.id, c.name ORDER BY last_order_date ASC NULLS FIRST LIMIT 1
expected_result_check: len(results) == 1 and 'customer_id' in results[0]

question: Which sub-categories are growing fastest year over year?
expected_sql: WITH yearly AS (SELECT p.sub_category, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM o.order_date)::INT AS yr, SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id JOIN products p ON p.id = oi.product_id GROUP BY 1, 2) SELECT y2.sub_category, ROUND(((y2.revenue - y1.revenue) / NULLIF(y1.revenue, 0)) * 100, 2) AS yoy_growth_pct FROM yearly y2 JOIN yearly y1 ON y2.sub_category = y1.sub_category AND y2.yr = y1.yr + 1 ORDER BY yoy_growth_pct DESC LIMIT 15
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('yoy_growth_pct' in r for r in results)

question: What percent of orders include any discount?
expected_sql: WITH order_discount AS (SELECT oi.order_id, MAX(CASE WHEN oi.discount > 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS has_discount FROM order_items oi GROUP BY oi.order_id) SELECT ROUND((SUM(has_discount)::NUMERIC / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0)) * 100, 2) AS discounted_order_pct FROM order_discount
expected_result_check: len(results) == 1 and 'discounted_order_pct' in results[0]

question: Show monthly orders and revenue by customer segment
expected_sql: SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', o.order_date) AS month, c.segment, COUNT(DISTINCT o.id) AS order_count, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price * (1 - oi.discount / 100.0)), 2) AS revenue FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON c.id = o.customer_id JOIN order_items oi ON oi.order_id = o.id GROUP BY 1, 2 ORDER BY 1, 2
expected_result_check: len(results) >= 1 and all('order_count' in r and 'revenue' in r for r in results)
